is a [British television](/wiki/British_television "British television") production company which has produced drama programming for many broadcasters.[[1]](#cite_note-1) It was set up in 1998 by [Charles Pattinson](/wiki/Charles_Pattinson "Charles Pattinson") and [George Faber](/wiki/George_S._J._Faber "George S. J. Faber"), colleagues at [BBC Films](/wiki/BBC_Films "BBC Films"). Their first film was *[Morvern Callar](/wiki/Morvern_Callar_(film) "Morvern Callar (film)")*, which was credited as a co-production with BBC Films as they had begun developing it while still employed there.[[2]](#cite_note-2) In 2003 Company Pictures became part of [All3Media](/wiki/All3Media "All3Media").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

Jake Kanter, [Company founders quit - to set up new All3 indie](http://www.broadcastnow.co.uk/news/indies/company-founders-quit-to-set-up-new-all3-indie/5043614.article), *[Broadcast](/wiki/Broadcast_(magazine) "Broadcast (magazine)")*, 22 June 2012. Accessed 25 October 2016.

Jake Kanter, [John Yorke leaves Company Pictures](http://www.broadcastnow.co.uk/news/john-yorke-leaves-company-pictures/5085814.article), *[Broadcast](/wiki/Broadcast_(magazine) "Broadcast (magazine)")*, 27 March 2015. Accessed 25 October 2016.

[Michele Buck to head Company Pictures](http://www.broadcastnow.co.uk/news/michele-buck-to-head-company-pictures/5089513.article), *[Broadcast](/wiki/Broadcast_(magazine) "Broadcast (magazine)")*, 18 June 2015. Accessed 25 October 2016.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

British television production company

Company Pictures

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Company type | [Subsidiary](/wiki/Subsidiary "Subsidiary") |
| Industry | [Film](/wiki/Film_industry "Film industry") [Television](/wiki/Television_industry "Television industry") |
| Founded | 1998; 28 years ago (1998) |
| Founders | [Charles Pattinson](/wiki/Charles_Pattinson "Charles Pattinson") [George Faber](/wiki/George_S._J._Faber "George S. J. Faber") |
| Headquarters | [London](/wiki/London "London") , United Kingdom |
| Key people | Michele Buck (CEO) |
| Products | [Motion pictures](/wiki/Film "Film") [Television programmes](/wiki/Television_program "Television program") |
| [Parent](/wiki/Parent_company "Parent company") | [All3Media](/wiki/All3Media "All3Media") (2003–present) |
| Website | [www.companypictures.co.uk](http://www.companypictures.co.uk) |

**Company Pictures** is a [British television](/wiki/British_television "British television") production company which has produced drama programming for many broadcasters.[[1]](#cite_note-1) It was set up in 1998 by [Charles Pattinson](/wiki/Charles_Pattinson "Charles Pattinson") and [George Faber](/wiki/George_S._J._Faber "George S. J. Faber"), colleagues at [BBC Films](/wiki/BBC_Films "BBC Films"). Their first film was *[Morvern Callar](/wiki/Morvern_Callar_(film) "Morvern Callar (film)")*, which was credited as a co-production with BBC Films as they had begun developing it while still employed there.[[2]](#cite_note-2) In 2003 Company Pictures became part of [All3Media](/wiki/All3Media "All3Media").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

In 2012, founders Pattinson and Faber left Company Pictures to set up another independent production company,[[4]](#cite_note-4) and [John Yorke](/wiki/John_Yorke_(producer) "John Yorke (producer)") became managing director until 2015.[[5]](#cite_note-5) He was succeeded by Michele Buck, former joint managing director of [Mammoth Screen](/wiki/Mammoth_Screen "Mammoth Screen").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

For both artistic and fiscal reasons, when producing the TV series *[The White Queen](/wiki/The_White_Queen_(TV_series) "The White Queen (TV series)")*, Company Pictures entered into collaboration with a Flemish production house. This enabled them to film in the medieval cities of [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent") and [Bruges](/wiki/Bruges "Bruges"), while receiving tax-breaks as a European co-production and for investment in Flanders.[[7]](#cite_note-7)
